NAME

audio.c - 🔊 Audio capture and playback using PortAudio with buffer management

SYNOPSIS

Macros


#define WORKER_BATCH_FRAMES 128
#define WORKER_BATCH_SAMPLES (WORKER_BATCH_FRAMES * AUDIO_CHANNELS)
#define WORKER_TIMEOUT_MS 1

asciichat_error_t audio_write_samples (audio_context_t *ctx, const float *buffer, int num_samples)

asciichat_error_t audio_list_output_devices (audio_device_info_t **out_devices, unsigned int *out_count)

asciichat_error_t audio_validate_batch_params (const audio_batch_info_t *batch)

asciichat_error_t audio_dequantize_samples (const uint8_t * samples_ptr, uint32_t total_samples, float * out_samples)

Definition at line 1914 of file lib/audio/audio.c.

1914                                                                                                                    {
1915   if (!samples_ptr || !out_samples || total_samples == 0) {
1916     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters for audio dequantization");
1917   }
1918 
1919   for (uint32_t i = 0; i < total_samples; i++) {
1920     uint32_t network_sample;
1921     // Use memcpy to safely handle potential misalignment from packet header
1922     memcpy(&network_sample, samples_ptr + i * sizeof(uint32_t), sizeof(uint32_t));
1923     int32_t scaled = (int32_t)NET_TO_HOST_U32(network_sample);
1924     out_samples[i] = (float)scaled / 2147483647.0f;
1925   }
1926 
1927   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1928 }

void
  audio_destroy (audio_context_t * ctx)

1285                                          {
1286   if (!ctx) {
1287     return;
1288   }
1289 
1290   // Always release PortAudio refcount if it was incremented
1291   // audio_init() calls Pa_Initialize() very early, and if it fails partway through,
1292   // ctx->initialized will be false. But we MUST still call audio_release_portaudio()
1293   // to properly decrement the refcount and allow Pa_Terminate() to be called.
1294   if (ctx->initialized) {
1295 
1296     // Stop duplex stream if running (this also stops the worker thread)
1297     if (ctx->running) {
1298       audio_stop_duplex(ctx);
1299     }
1300 
1301     // Ensure worker thread is stopped even if streams weren't running
1302     if (ctx->worker_running) {
1303       log_debug("Stopping worker thread during audio_destroy");
1304       atomic_store_bool(&ctx->worker_should_stop, true);
1305       cond_signal(&ctx->worker_cond); // Wake up worker if waiting
1306       asciichat_thread_join(&ctx->worker_thread, NULL);
1307       ctx->worker_running = false;
1308     }
1309 
1310     mutex_lock(&ctx->state_mutex);

1316     audio_ring_buffer_destroy(ctx->raw_render_rb);
1317     audio_ring_buffer_destroy(ctx->processed_playback_rb);
1318     audio_ring_buffer_destroy(ctx->render_buffer); // May be NULL, that's OK
1319 
1320     // Free pre-allocated worker buffers
1321     SAFE_FREE(ctx->worker_capture_batch);
1322     SAFE_FREE(ctx->worker_render_batch);
1323     SAFE_FREE(ctx->worker_playback_batch);
1324

1327     mutex_destroy(&ctx->worker_mutex);
1328

1330 
1331     mutex_unlock(&ctx->state_mutex);

1333

1260 
1261   ctx->initialized = true;
1262   atomic_store_bool(&ctx->shutting_down, false);
1263 
1264   /* Register audio context with named registry */
1265   NAMED_REGISTER(ctx, "audio_context", "audio_context_t", "0x%tx", NULL);
1266 
1267   /* Register audio context's sync primitives with hierarchical naming */
1268   NAMED_REGISTER_MUTEX(&ctx->state_mutex, "state_mutex", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(ctx));
1269   NAMED_REGISTER_MUTEX(&ctx->worker_mutex, "worker_mutex", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(ctx));
1270   NAMED_REGISTER_COND(&ctx->worker_cond, "worker_cond", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(ctx));
1271   NAMED_REGISTER_ATOMIC(&ctx->worker_should_stop, "worker_should_stop", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(ctx));
1272   NAMED_REGISTER_ATOMIC(&ctx->shutting_down, "shutting_down", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(ctx));
1273 
1274   /* Register ring buffers as child structures with audio context as parent */
1275   NAMED_REGISTER_AUDIO_RINGBUF(ctx->capture_buffer, "capture_buffer", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(ctx));
1276   NAMED_REGISTER_AUDIO_RINGBUF(ctx->playback_buffer, "playback_buffer", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(ctx));
1277   NAMED_REGISTER_AUDIO_RINGBUF(ctx->raw_capture_rb, "raw_capture_rb", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(ctx));
1278   NAMED_REGISTER_AUDIO_RINGBUF(ctx->raw_render_rb, "raw_render_rb", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(ctx));
1279   NAMED_REGISTER_AUDIO_RINGBUF(ctx->processed_playback_rb, "processed_playback_rb", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(ctx));
1280 
1281   log_info("Audio system initialized successfully (worker thread architecture enabled)");
1282   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1283 }

bool audio_is_supported_sample_rate (uint32_t
  sample_rate)


Definition at line 2010 of file
  lib/audio/audio.c.


2010                                                           {
2011   // List of commonly supported audio sample rates
2012   static const uint32_t supported_rates[] = {
2013       8000,   // Telephone quality
2014       16000,  // Wideband telephony
2015       24000,  // High quality speech
2016       32000,  // Good for video
2017       44100,  // CD quality (less common in VoIP)
2018       48000,  // Standard professional
2019       96000,  // High-end professional
2020       192000, // Ultra-high-end mastering
2021   };
2022 
2023   const size_t rate_count = sizeof(supported_rates) / sizeof(supported_rates[0]);
2024   for (size_t i = 0; i < rate_count; i++) {
2025     if (sample_rate == supported_rates[i]) {
2026       return true;
2027     }
2028   }
2029 
2030   return false;
2031 }

844                                                       {
845   if (!rb)
846     return;
847 
848   mutex_lock(&rb->mutex);
849   // Reset buffer to empty state (no audio to play = silence at shutdown)
850   atomic_store_u64(&rb->write_index, 0);
851   atomic_store_u64(&rb->read_index, 0);
852   rb->last_sample = 0.0f;
853   // Clear the actual data to zeros to prevent any stale audio
854   SAFE_MEMSET(rb->data, sizeof(rb->data), 0, sizeof(rb->data));

size_t
  audio_ring_buffer_peek (audio_ring_buffer_t * rb, float * data, size_t
  samples)


Peek at available samples without consuming them (for AEC3 render
    signal). This function reads samples from the jitter buffer WITHOUT
    advancing the read_index. Used to feed audio to AEC3 for echo cancellation
    even during jitter buffer fill period.




Parameters


rb Ring buffer to peek from


data Output buffer for samples


samples Number of samples to peek


Returns


Number of samples actually peeked (may be less than
  requested)


Definition at line 1071 of file
  lib/audio/audio.c.


1071                                                                                     {
1072   if (!rb || !data || samples <= 0) {
1073     return 0;
1074   }
1075 
1076   // LOCK-FREE: Load indices with proper memory ordering
1077   unsigned int write_idx = atomic_load_u64(&rb->write_index);
1078   unsigned int read_idx = atomic_load_u64(&rb->read_index);
1079 
1080   // Calculate available samples
1081   size_t available;
1082   if (write_idx >= read_idx) {
1083     available = write_idx - read_idx;
1084   } else {
1085     available = AUDIO_RING_BUFFER_SIZE - read_idx + write_idx;
1086   }
1087 
1088   size_t to_peek = (samples > available) ? available : samples;
1089 
1090   if (to_peek == 0) {
1091     return 0;
1092   }
1093 
1094   // Copy samples in chunks (handle wraparound)
1095   size_t first_chunk = (read_idx + to_peek <= AUDIO_RING_BUFFER_SIZE) ? to_peek : (AUDIO_RING_BUFFER_SIZE - read_idx);
1096 
1097   SAFE_MEMCPY(data, first_chunk * sizeof(float), rb->data + read_idx, first_chunk * sizeof(float));
1098 
1099   if (first_chunk < to_peek) {
1100     // Wraparound: copy second chunk from beginning of buffer
1101     size_t second_chunk = to_peek - first_chunk;
1102     SAFE_MEMCPY(data + first_chunk, second_chunk * sizeof(float), rb->data, second_chunk * sizeof(float));
1103   }
1104 
1105   return to_peek;
1106 }






References atomic_load_u64().






size_t
  audio_ring_buffer_read (audio_ring_buffer_t * rb, float * data, size_t
  samples)

  lib/audio/audio.c.


941                                                                                     {
942   if (!rb || !data || samples <= 0) {
943     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters: rb=%p, data=%p, samples=%d", rb, data, samples);
944     return 0; // Return 0 samples read on error
945   }
946 
947   // LOCK-FREE: Load indices with proper memory ordering
948   // - Load writer's write_index with acquire (see writer's data updates)
949   // - Load our own read_index with relaxed (no sync needed with ourselves)
950   unsigned int write_idx = atomic_load_u64(&rb->write_index);
951   unsigned int read_idx = atomic_load_u64(&rb->read_index);
952 
953   // Calculate available samples
954   size_t available;
955   if (write_idx >= read_idx) {
956     available = write_idx - read_idx;
957   } else {
958     available = AUDIO_RING_BUFFER_SIZE - read_idx + write_idx;
959   }
960 
961   // LOCK-FREE: Load jitter buffer state with acquire ordering
962   bool jitter_filled = atomic_load_u64(&rb->jitter_buffer_filled);
963   int crossfade_remaining = atomic_load_u64(&rb->crossfade_samples_remaining);
964   bool fade_in = atomic_load_u64(&rb->crossfade_fade_in);
965 
966   // Jitter buffer: don't read until initial fill threshold is reached
967   // (only for playback buffers - capture buffers have jitter_buffer_enabled = false)
968   if (!jitter_filled && rb->jitter_buffer_enabled) {
969     // Check if we've accumulated enough samples to start playback
970     if (available >= AUDIO_JITTER_BUFFER_THRESHOLD) {
971       atomic_store_u64(&rb->jitter_buffer_filled, true);
972       atomic_store_u64(&rb->crossfade_samples_remaining, AUDIO_CROSSFADE_SAMPLES);
973       atomic_store_u64(&rb->crossfade_fade_in, true);
974       log_info("Jitter buffer filled (%zu samples), starting playback with fade-in", available);
975       // Reload state for processing below
976       jitter_filled = true;
977       crossfade_remaining = AUDIO_CROSSFADE_SAMPLES;
978       fade_in = true;
979     } else {
980       // Log buffer fill progress every second
981       log_debug_every(NS_PER_MS_INT, "Jitter buffer filling: %zu/%d samples (%.1f%%)", available,
982                       AUDIO_JITTER_BUFFER_THRESHOLD, (100.0f * available) / AUDIO_JITTER_BUFFER_THRESHOLD);
983       return 0; // Return 0 samples - caller will pad with silence
984     }
985   }
986 
987   // Periodic buffer health logging (every 5 seconds when healthy)
988   unsigned int underruns = atomic_load_u64(&rb->underrun_count);
989   log_dev_every(5 * NS_PER_MS_INT, "Buffer health: %zu/%d samples (%.1f%%), underruns=%u", available,
990                 AUDIO_RING_BUFFER_SIZE, (100.0f * available) / AUDIO_RING_BUFFER_SIZE, underruns);
991 
992   // Low buffer handling: DON'T pause playback - continue reading what's available
993   // and fill the rest with silence. Pausing causes a feedback loop where:
994   // 1. Underrun -> pause reading -> buffer overflows from incoming samples
995   // 2. Threshold reached -> resume reading -> drains too fast -> underrun again
996   //
997   // Instead: always consume samples to prevent overflow, use silence for missing data
998   if (rb->jitter_buffer_enabled && available < AUDIO_JITTER_LOW_WATER_MARK) {
999     unsigned int underrun_count = atomic_fetch_add_u64(&rb->underrun_count, 1) + 1;
1000     log_warn_every(LOG_RATE_FAST,
1001                    "Audio buffer low #%u: only %zu samples available (low water mark: %d), padding with silence",
1002                    underrun_count, available, AUDIO_JITTER_LOW_WATER_MARK);
1003     // Don't set jitter_buffer_filled = false - keep reading to prevent overflow
1004   }
1005 
1006   size_t to_read = (samples > available) ? available : samples;
1007 
1008   // Optimize: copy in chunks instead of one sample at a time
1009   size_t remaining = AUDIO_RING_BUFFER_SIZE - read_idx;
1010 
1011   if (to_read <= remaining) {
1012     // Can copy in one chunk
1013     SAFE_MEMCPY(data, to_read * sizeof(float), &rb->data[read_idx], to_read * sizeof(float));
1014   } else {
1015     // Need to wrap around - copy in two chunks
1016     SAFE_MEMCPY(data, remaining * sizeof(float), &rb->data[read_idx], remaining * sizeof(float));
1017     SAFE_MEMCPY(&data[remaining], (to_read - remaining) * sizeof(float), &rb->data[0],
1018                 (to_read - remaining) * sizeof(float));
1019   }
1020 
1021   // LOCK-FREE: Store new read_index with release ordering
1022   // This ensures all data reads above complete before the index update
1023   unsigned int new_read_idx = (read_idx + (unsigned int)to_read) % AUDIO_RING_BUFFER_SIZE;
1024   atomic_store_u64(&rb->read_index, new_read_idx);
1025 
1026   // Apply fade-in if recovering from underrun
1027   if (fade_in && crossfade_remaining > 0) {
1028     int fade_start = AUDIO_CROSSFADE_SAMPLES - crossfade_remaining;
1029     size_t fade_samples = (to_read < (size_t)crossfade_remaining) ? to_read : (size_t)crossfade_remaining;
1030 
1031     for (size_t i = 0; i < fade_samples; i++) {
1032       float fade_factor = (float)(fade_start + (int)i + 1) / (float)AUDIO_CROSSFADE_SAMPLES;
1033       data[i] *= fade_factor;
1034     }
1035 
1036     int new_crossfade_remaining = crossfade_remaining - (int)fade_samples;
1037     atomic_store_u64(&rb->crossfade_samples_remaining, new_crossfade_remaining);
1038     if (new_crossfade_remaining <= 0) {
1039       atomic_store_u64(&rb->crossfade_fade_in, false);
1040       log_debug("Audio fade-in complete");
1041     }
1042   }
1043 
1044   // Save last sample for potential fade-out
1045   // Note: only update if we actually read some data
1046   // This is NOT atomic - only the reader thread writes this
1047   if (to_read > 0) {
1048     rb->last_sample = data[to_read - 1];
1049   }
1050 
1051   // Return ACTUAL number of samples read, not padded count
1052   // The caller (mixer) expects truthful return values to detect underruns
1053   // and handle silence padding externally. Internal padding creates double-padding bugs.
1054   //
1055   // This function was incorrectly returning `samples` even when
1056   // it only read `to_read` samples. This broke the mixer's underrun detection.
1057   return to_read;
1058 }

858                                                                                                    {
859   if (!rb || !data || samples <= 0)
860     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters: rb=%p, data=%p, samples=%d", rb, data, samples);
861 
862   // Validate samples doesn't exceed our buffer size
863   if (samples > AUDIO_RING_BUFFER_SIZE) {
864     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_BUFFER, "Attempted to write %d samples, but buffer size is only %d", samples,
865                      AUDIO_RING_BUFFER_SIZE);
866   }
867 
868   // LOCK-FREE: Load indices with proper memory ordering
869   // - Load our own write_index with relaxed (no sync needed with ourselves)
870   // - Load reader's read_index with acquire (see reader's updates to free space)
871   unsigned int write_idx = atomic_load_u64(&rb->write_index);
872   unsigned int read_idx = atomic_load_u64(&rb->read_index);
873 
874   // Calculate current buffer level (how many samples are buffered)
875   int buffer_level;
876   if (write_idx >= read_idx) {
877     buffer_level = (int)(write_idx - read_idx);
878   } else {
879     buffer_level = AUDIO_RING_BUFFER_SIZE - (int)(read_idx - write_idx);
880   }
881   // Reserve 1 slot to distinguish between full and empty states
882   // (when buffer is full, write_idx will be just before read_idx, not equal to it)
883   int available = AUDIO_RING_BUFFER_SIZE - 1 - buffer_level;
884 
885   // HIGH WATER MARK: Drop INCOMING samples to prevent latency accumulation
886   // Writer must not modify read_index (race condition with reader).
887   // Instead, we drop incoming samples to keep buffer bounded.
888   // This sacrifices newest data to prevent unbounded latency growth.
889   if (buffer_level > AUDIO_JITTER_HIGH_WATER_MARK) {
890     // Buffer is already too full - drop incoming samples to maintain target level
891     int target_writes = AUDIO_JITTER_TARGET_LEVEL - buffer_level;
892     if (target_writes < 0) {
893       target_writes = 0; // Buffer is way over - drop everything
894     }
895 
896     if (samples > target_writes) {
897       int dropped = samples - target_writes;
898       log_warn_every(LOG_RATE_FAST,
899                      "Audio buffer high water mark exceeded (%d > %d): dropping %d INCOMING samples "
900                      "(keeping newest %d to maintain target %d)",
901                      buffer_level, AUDIO_JITTER_HIGH_WATER_MARK, dropped, target_writes, AUDIO_JITTER_TARGET_LEVEL);
902       samples = target_writes; // Only write what fits within target level
903     }
904   }
905 
906   // Now write the new samples - should always have enough space after above
907   int samples_to_write = samples;
908   if (samples > available) {
909     // This should rarely happen after the high water mark logic above
910     int samples_dropped = samples - available;
911     samples_to_write = available;
912     log_warn_every(LOG_RATE_FAST, "Audio buffer overflow: dropping %d of %d incoming samples (buffer_used=%d/%d)",
913                    samples_dropped, samples, AUDIO_RING_BUFFER_SIZE - available, AUDIO_RING_BUFFER_SIZE);
914   }
915 
916   // Write only the samples that fit (preserves existing data integrity)
917   if (samples_to_write > 0) {
918     int remaining = AUDIO_RING_BUFFER_SIZE - (int)write_idx;
919 
920     if (samples_to_write <= remaining) {
921       // Can copy in one chunk
922       SAFE_MEMCPY(&rb->data[write_idx], samples_to_write * sizeof(float), data, samples_to_write * sizeof(float));
923     } else {
924       // Need to wrap around - copy in two chunks
925       SAFE_MEMCPY(&rb->data[write_idx], remaining * sizeof(float), data, remaining * sizeof(float));
926       SAFE_MEMCPY(&rb->data[0], (samples_to_write - remaining) * sizeof(float), &data[remaining],
927                   (samples_to_write - remaining) * sizeof(float));
928     }
929 
930     // LOCK-FREE: Store new write_index with release ordering
931     // This ensures all data writes above are visible before the index update
932     unsigned int new_write_idx = (write_idx + (unsigned int)samples_to_write) % AUDIO_RING_BUFFER_SIZE;
933     atomic_store_u64(&rb->write_index, new_write_idx);
934   }
935 
936   // Note: jitter buffer fill check is now done in read function for better control
937 
938   return ASCIICHAT_OK; // Success
939 }

1403

1421

1428       outputParams.suggestedLatency = outputInfo->defaultLowOutputLatency;

1432     }
1433   }
1434

1438 
1439   log_debug("Opening audio:");
1440   if (has_input) {
1441     log_info("  Input:  %s (%.0f Hz)", inputInfo->name, inputInfo->defaultSampleRate);
1442   } else if (ctx->playback_only) {
1443     log_debug("  Input:  (playback-only mode - no microphone)");
1444   } else {
1445     log_debug("  Input:  (none)");
1446   }
1447   if (has_output) {
1448     log_info("  Output: %s (%.0f Hz)", outputInfo->name, outputInfo->defaultSampleRate);
1449   } else {
1450     log_debug("  Output: None (input-only mode - will send audio to server)");
1451   }
1452 
1453   // Check if sample rates differ - ALSA full-duplex doesn't handle this well
1454   // If no input or no output, always use separate streams
1455   bool rates_differ = has_input && has_output && (inputInfo->defaultSampleRate != outputInfo->defaultSampleRate);
1456   bool try_separate = rates_differ || !has_input || !has_output;
1457   PaError err = paNoError;
1458 
1459   if (!try_separate) {
1460     // Try full-duplex first (preferred - perfect AEC3 timing)
1461     err = Pa_OpenStream(&ctx->duplex_stream, &inputParams, &outputParams, AUDIO_SAMPLE_RATE, AUDIO_FRAMES_PER_BUFFER,
1462                         paClipOff, duplex_callback, ctx);
1463 
1464     if (err == paNoError) {
1465       err = Pa_StartStream(ctx->duplex_stream);
1466       if (err != paNoError) {
1467         Pa_CloseStream(ctx->duplex_stream);
1468         ctx->duplex_stream = NULL;
1469         log_warn("Full-duplex stream failed to start: %s", Pa_GetErrorText(err));
1470         try_separate = true;
1471       }
1472     } else {
1473       log_warn("Full-duplex stream failed to open: %s", Pa_GetErrorText(err));
1474       try_separate = true;
1475     }
1476   }
1477 
1478   if (try_separate) {
1479     // Fall back to separate streams (needed when sample rates differ or input-only/playback-only mode)
1480     if (has_output && has_input) {
1481       log_info("Using separate input/output streams (sample rates differ: %.0f vs %.0f Hz)",
1482                inputInfo->defaultSampleRate, outputInfo->defaultSampleRate);
1483       log_info("  Will resample: buffer at %.0f Hz → output at %.0f Hz", (double)AUDIO_SAMPLE_RATE,
1484                outputInfo->defaultSampleRate);
1485     } else if (has_output) {
1486       log_debug("Using output-only mode (playback-only for mirror/media)");
1487     } else if (has_input) {
1488       log_info("Using input-only mode (no output device available)");
1489     }
1490 
1491     // Store the internal sample rate (buffer rate)
1492     ctx->sample_rate = AUDIO_SAMPLE_RATE;
1493 
1494     // Create render buffer for AEC3 reference synchronization
1495     ctx->render_buffer = audio_ring_buffer_create_for_capture();
1496     if (!ctx->render_buffer) {
1497       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create render buffer");
1498     }
1499 
1500     // Open output stream only if output device exists
1501     bool output_ok = false;
1502     double actual_output_rate = 0;
1503     if (has_output) {
1504       // Try to use AUDIO_SAMPLE_RATE (48kHz) first for best quality and duplex compatibility
1505       // Fall back to native rate if 48kHz not supported
1506       double preferred_rate = AUDIO_SAMPLE_RATE;
1507       double native_rate = outputInfo->defaultSampleRate;
1508 
1509       log_debug("Attempting output at %.0f Hz (preferred) vs %.0f Hz (native)", preferred_rate, native_rate);
1510 
1511       // Always use output_callback for output streams (both output-only and duplex modes)
1512       // PortAudio will invoke the callback whenever it needs audio data
1513       // The callback reads from media_source (mirror mode) or playback buffers (network mode)
1514       PaStreamCallback *callback = output_callback;
1515 
1516       // Try preferred rate first
1517       err = Pa_OpenStream(&ctx->output_stream, NULL, &outputParams, preferred_rate, AUDIO_FRAMES_PER_BUFFER, paClipOff,
1518                           callback, ctx);
1519 
1520       if (err == paNoError) {
1521         actual_output_rate = preferred_rate;
1522         output_ok = true;
1523         log_info("✓ Output opened at preferred rate: %.0f Hz (matches input - optimal!)", preferred_rate);
1524       } else {
1525         log_warn("Failed to open output at %.0f Hz: %s, trying native rate %.0f Hz", preferred_rate,
1526                  Pa_GetErrorText(err), native_rate);
1527 
1528         // If first Pa_OpenStream call left a partial stream, clean it up before retrying
1529         if (ctx->output_stream) {
1530           log_debug("Closing partially-opened output stream from failed preferred rate");
1531           Pa_CloseStream(ctx->output_stream);
1532           ctx->output_stream = NULL;
1533         }
1534 
1535         // Fall back to native rate (still using blocking mode for output-only)
1536         err = Pa_OpenStream(&ctx->output_stream, NULL, &outputParams, native_rate, AUDIO_FRAMES_PER_BUFFER, paClipOff,
1537                             callback, ctx);
1538 
1539         if (err == paNoError) {
1540           actual_output_rate = native_rate;
1541           output_ok = true;
1542           log_info("✓ Output opened at native rate: %.0f Hz (will need resampling)", native_rate);
1543         } else {
1544           log_warn("Failed to open output stream at native rate: %s", Pa_GetErrorText(err));
1545           // Clean up if fallback also failed
1546           if (ctx->output_stream) {
1547             log_debug("Closing partially-opened output stream from failed native rate");
1548             Pa_CloseStream(ctx->output_stream);
1549             ctx->output_stream = NULL;
1550           }
1551         }
1552       }
1553 
1554       // Store actual output rate for resampling
1555       if (output_ok) {
1556         ctx->output_device_rate = actual_output_rate;
1557         if (actual_output_rate != AUDIO_SAMPLE_RATE) {
1558           log_warn("⚠️  Output rate mismatch: %.0f Hz output vs %.0f Hz input - resampling will be used",
1559                    actual_output_rate, (double)AUDIO_SAMPLE_RATE);
1560         }
1561       }
1562     }
1563 
1564     // Open input stream only if we have input (skip for playback-only mode)
1565     bool input_ok = !has_input; // If no input, mark as OK (skip)
1566     if (has_input) {
1567       // Use pipeline sample rate (AUDIO_SAMPLE_RATE)
1568       // In input-only mode, we don't need to match output device rate
1569       double input_stream_rate = AUDIO_SAMPLE_RATE;
1570       err = Pa_OpenStream(&ctx->input_stream, &inputParams, NULL, input_stream_rate, AUDIO_FRAMES_PER_BUFFER, paClipOff,
1571                           input_callback, ctx);
1572       input_ok = (err == paNoError);
1573 
1574       // If input failed, try device 0 as fallback (HDMI on BeaglePlay)
1575       if (!input_ok) {
1576         log_debug("Input failed - trying device 0 as fallback");
1577 
1578         // Clean up partial stream from first attempt before retrying
1579         if (ctx->input_stream) {
1580           log_debug("Closing partially-opened input stream from failed primary device");
1581           Pa_CloseStream(ctx->input_stream);
1582           ctx->input_stream = NULL;
1583         }
1584 
1585         PaStreamParameters fallback_input_params = inputParams;
1586         fallback_input_params.device = 0;
1587         const PaDeviceInfo *device_0_info = Pa_GetDeviceInfo(0);
1588         if (device_0_info && device_0_info->maxInputChannels > 0) {
1589           err = Pa_OpenStream(&ctx->input_stream, &fallback_input_params, NULL, input_stream_rate,
1590                               AUDIO_FRAMES_PER_BUFFER, paClipOff, input_callback, ctx);
1591           if (err == paNoError) {
1592             log_info("Input stream opened on device 0 (fallback from default)");
1593             input_ok = true;
1594           } else {
1595             log_warn("Fallback also failed on device 0: %s", Pa_GetErrorText(err));
1596             // Clean up if fallback also failed
1597             if (ctx->input_stream) {
1598               log_debug("Closing partially-opened input stream from failed fallback device");
1599               Pa_CloseStream(ctx->input_stream);
1600               ctx->input_stream = NULL;
1601             }
1602           }
1603         }
1604       }
1605 
1606       if (!input_ok) {
1607         log_warn("Failed to open input stream: %s", Pa_GetErrorText(err));
1608       }
1609     }
1610 
1611     // Check if we got at least one stream working
1612     if (!input_ok && !output_ok) {
1613       // Neither stream works - fail completely
1614       audio_ring_buffer_destroy(ctx->render_buffer);
1615       ctx->render_buffer = NULL;
1616       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_AUDIO, "Failed to open both input and output streams");
1617     }
1618 
1619     // If output failed but input works, we can still send audio to server
1620     if (!output_ok && input_ok) {
1621       log_info("Output stream unavailable - continuing with input-only (can send audio to server)");
1622       ctx->output_stream = NULL;
1623     }
1624     // If input failed but output works, we can still receive audio from server
1625     if (!input_ok && output_ok) {
1626       log_info("Input stream unavailable - continuing with output-only (can receive audio from server)");
1627       ctx->input_stream = NULL;
1628     }
1629 
1630     // Start output stream if it's open
1631     if (ctx->output_stream) {
1632       err = Pa_StartStream(ctx->output_stream);
1633       if (err != paNoError) {
1634         if (ctx->input_stream)
1635           Pa_CloseStream(ctx->input_stream);
1636         Pa_CloseStream(ctx->output_stream);
1637         ctx->input_stream = NULL;
1638         ctx->output_stream = NULL;
1639         audio_ring_buffer_destroy(ctx->render_buffer);
1640         ctx->render_buffer = NULL;
1641         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_AUDIO, "Failed to start output stream: %s", Pa_GetErrorText(err));
1642       }
1643     }
1644 
1645     // Start input stream if it's open
1646     if (ctx->input_stream) {
1647       err = Pa_StartStream(ctx->input_stream);
1648       if (err != paNoError) {
1649         if (ctx->output_stream)
1650           Pa_StopStream(ctx->output_stream);
1651         if (ctx->input_stream)
1652           Pa_CloseStream(ctx->input_stream);
1653         if (ctx->output_stream)
1654           Pa_CloseStream(ctx->output_stream);
1655         ctx->input_stream = NULL;
1656         ctx->output_stream = NULL;
1657         audio_ring_buffer_destroy(ctx->render_buffer);
1658         ctx->render_buffer = NULL;
1659         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_AUDIO, "Failed to start input stream: %s", Pa_GetErrorText(err));
1660       }
1661     }
1662 
1663     ctx->separate_streams = true;
1664     log_debug("Separate streams started successfully");
1665   } else {
1666     ctx->separate_streams = false;
1667     log_info("Full-duplex stream started (single callback, perfect AEC3 timing)");
1668   }
1669 
1670   audio_set_realtime_priority();
1671 
1672   // Start worker thread for heavy audio processing
1673   if (!ctx->worker_running) {
1674     atomic_store_bool(&ctx->worker_should_stop, false);
1675     if (asciichat_thread_create(&ctx->worker_thread, "audio_worker", audio_worker_thread, ctx) != 0) {
1676       // Failed to create worker thread - stop streams and cleanup
1677       if (ctx->duplex_stream) {
1678         Pa_StopStream(ctx->duplex_stream);
1679         Pa_CloseStream(ctx->duplex_stream);
1680         ctx->duplex_stream = NULL;
1681       }
1682       if (ctx->input_stream) {
1683         Pa_StopStream(ctx->input_stream);
1684         Pa_CloseStream(ctx->input_stream);
1685         ctx->input_stream = NULL;
1686       }
1687       if (ctx->output_stream) {
1688         Pa_StopStream(ctx->output_stream);
1689         Pa_CloseStream(ctx->output_stream);
1690         ctx->output_stream = NULL;
1691       }
1692       audio_ring_buffer_destroy(ctx->render_buffer);
1693       ctx->render_buffer = NULL;
1694       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_THREAD, "Failed to create worker thread");
1695     }
1696     ctx->worker_running = true;
1697     log_debug("Worker thread started successfully");
1698   }
1699 
1700   // Lock to update final state flags atomically
1701   mutex_lock(&ctx->state_mutex);
1702   ctx->running = true;
1703   ctx->sample_rate = AUDIO_SAMPLE_RATE;
1704   mutex_unlock(&ctx->state_mutex);
1705   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1706 }

void
  audio_terminate_portaudio_final (void )


Terminate PortAudio and free all device resources. This must be
    called to actually free device structures allocated by ALSA/libpulse. It
    should be called AFTER all audio contexts are destroyed and session cleanup
    is complete.


Definition at line 87 of file lib/audio/audio.c.

92 
93     log_debug("[PORTAUDIO_TERM] Pa_Terminate() returned: %s", Pa_GetErrorText(err));
94   }
95 }

  audio_validate_batch_params (const audio_batch_info_t * batch)


Definition at line 1969 of file

1969                                                                                {
1970   if (!batch) {
1971     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Audio batch info pointer is NULL");
1972   }
1973 
1974   // Validate batch_count
1975   if (batch->batch_count == 0) {
1976     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Audio batch count cannot be zero");
1977   }
1978 
1979   // Check for reasonable max (256 frames per batch is very generous)
1980   if (batch->batch_count > 256) {
1981     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Audio batch count too large (batch_count=%u, max=256)", batch->batch_count);
1982   }
1983 
1984   // Validate channels (1=mono, 2=stereo, max 8 for multi-channel)
1985   if (batch->channels == 0 || batch->channels > 8) {
1986     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid channel count (channels=%u, valid=1-8)", batch->channels);
1987   }
1988 
1989   // Validate sample rate
1990   if (!audio_is_supported_sample_rate(batch->sample_rate)) {
1991     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Unsupported sample rate (sample_rate=%u)", batch->sample_rate);
1992   }
1993 
1994   // Check for reasonable sample counts
1995   if (batch->total_samples == 0) {
1996     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Audio batch has zero samples");
1997   }
1998 
1999   // Each batch typically has samples_per_frame worth of samples
2000   // For 48kHz at 20ms per frame: 48000 * 0.02 = 960 samples per frame
2001   // With max 256 frames, that's up to ~245k samples per batch
2002   if (batch->total_samples > 1000000) {
2003     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Audio batch sample count suspiciously large (total_samples=%u)",
2004                      batch->total_samples);
2005   }
2006 
2007   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2008 }

void
  resample_linear (const float * src, size_t src_samples, float * dst, size_t
  dst_samples, double src_rate, double dst_rate)


Simple linear interpolation resampler. Resamples from src_rate to
    dst_rate using linear interpolation.




Parameters


src Source samples at src_rate


src_samples Number of source samples


dst Destination buffer at dst_rate


dst_samples Number of destination samples to produce


src_rate Source sample rate (e.g., 48000)


dst_rate Destination sample rate (e.g., 44100)


Definition at line 539 of file
  lib/audio/audio.c.


540                                       {
541   if (src_samples == 0 || dst_samples == 0) {
542     SAFE_MEMSET(dst, dst_samples * sizeof(float), 0, dst_samples * sizeof(float));
543     return;
544   }
545 
546   double ratio = src_rate / dst_rate;
547 
548   for (size_t i = 0; i < dst_samples; i++) {
549     double src_pos = (double)i * ratio;
550     size_t idx0 = (size_t)src_pos;
551     size_t idx1 = idx0 + 1;
552     double frac = src_pos - (double)idx0;
553 
554     // Clamp indices to valid range
555     if (idx0 >= src_samples)
556       idx0 = src_samples - 1;
557     if (idx1 >= src_samples)
558       idx1 = src_samples - 1;
559 
560     // Linear interpolation
561     dst[i] = (float)((1.0 - frac) * src[idx0] + frac * src[idx1]);
562   }
563 }
